checks.a_analysis: The HTML output fails to represent the visual organization of the source PDF slide deck. The presentation relies on side-by-side comparisons (e.g., pages 12, 18, 23, 26) and visual examples to communicate its message. The HTML conversion linearizes everything into a single column of text and, critically, completely skips entire sections/slides that were purely visual (e.g., slides 12, 13, 15, 18, 20, 21, 23, 25, 26 are missing from the content flow). This significantly alters the organization and defeats the document's intent as a visual 'before and after' showcase.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains complex comparative layouts on pages 12, 18, 23, and 26, and informative visual-only slides on pages 13, 15, 20, 21, and 25.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output completely omits these comparison slides and sections, reducing the 29-page presentation to a truncated list of text headings and bullet points. Side-by-side comparative layouts are lost.

checks.a_analysis: There are no text-based data tables in the source PDF. Tables that appear in the document (e.g., on pages 16 and 18) are part of screenshots and thus are treated as images. As there are no HTML tables to evaluate, this check passes.

Almost all meaningful illustrative images and screenshots are missing from the HTML output. These are not decorative background images; they are the primary content of the presentation, providing visual proof of the website improvements discussed in the text. Key missing images include the phone evolution timeline (p. 8), PDF vs. HTML mockups (p. 9), WAVE tool results (p. 13), button contrast examples (p. 14), and numerous screenshots of the website's search results and forms (pp. 16, 17, 19, 22, 23, 24, 25).
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 8: 'Evolution of the Mobile Phone' graphic. Page 9: 'PDF vs HTML' graphic and mobile mockups. Page 14: Button contrast comparison images. Page 16: Search results table screenshot. Page 24: Finger touch target illustration.
checks.b_evidence_output: These meaningful images are entirely absent from the HTML rendering, leaving only the associated text headers and bullets without their visual context.

checks.a_analysis: No body content (paragraphs, tables, or sections) appears to be duplicated in the HTML output. Section headings appropriately appear in both the Table of Contents and the body of the document.
